skill.name: copywriting
skill.description: Write, rewrite, and improve marketing copy for homepages, landing pages, pricing pages, feature pages, product pages, and about pages.
skill.summary: Creates clear, credible, testable marketing copy from a confirmed copy brief.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography and reconnaissance patterns, but the cited lines are plain copywriting guidance in SKILL.md. No executable code, network access, filesystem access, shell commands, credential handling, or prompt injection attempts were found.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The cited lines contain copywriting metadata and ordinary marketing guidance, not cryptographic code or algorithm selection. No evidence found of hashing, encryption, key handling, or insecure cryptographic operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The signal is a substring-style false positive in prose-only content. The file has no code paths where cryptography could be performed.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Reconnaissance Language
low_findings.description: The cited lines are style and structure guidance for marketing copy. No evidence found of system enumeration, network scanning, host discovery, command execution, or data collection from the local environment.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious matches are ordinary copywriting instructions with no operational behavior. The skill contains no commands, URLs, scripts, or tool-use instructions for reconnaissance.

content.user_title: Write Conversion Copy With Clear Briefs
content.value_statement: Marketing pages often lose readers when the message is vague or unsupported. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to build a brief first, then produce structured and testable copy.
actual_capabilities: Collects page purpose, audience, offer, proof, and traffic context before writing.
Creates a copy brief summary and requires confirmation before drafting copy.
Writes structured page copy with headlines, subheadlines, CTAs, section headers, and body copy.
Provides two or three headline and CTA alternatives with brief rationale.
Documents assumptions and highlights missing proof instead of inventing claims.
Suggests copy elements that can be tested through focused experiments.
limitations: It does not verify product claims, statistics, testimonials, or legal compliance.
It requires user confirmation before moving from brief to final copy.
It does not create visual design, page layouts, analytics setup, or test implementation.
It works best when the user provides audience research, objections, and proof.

use_cases.title: Launch a Landing Page
use_cases.description: Create a clear page brief and first-draft conversion copy for a new campaign.

use_cases.title: Improve Product Messaging
use_cases.description: Rewrite feature and benefit sections so buyers understand the offer faster.

use_cases.title: Prepare Copy Tests
use_cases.description: Generate headline and CTA variants that isolate one message at a time.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Copy Brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new page.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a copy brief for a landing page. Ask for the page goal, audience, offer, proof, objections, traffic source, and primary CTA before writing copy.

prompt_templates.title: Rewrite a Page Section
prompt_templates.scenario: Improve existing copy with clearer benefits.
prompt_templates.prompt: Rewrite this page section using the skill. Keep the claim honest, connect each feature to a benefit, and provide two alternatives with rationale.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Full Page
prompt_templates.scenario: Create structured copy after the brief is confirmed.
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the confirmed brief below, write the full page copy with headline, subheadline, CTA, core sections, objection handling, final CTA, alternatives, and annotations.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Copy Experiments
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this page copy for testability. Identify the main value proposition, create headline and CTA variants, and recommend which message to test first.

output_examples.input: A founder needs homepage copy for a B2B scheduling tool but has not defined the audience.
output_examples.output: The skill first asks for the target role, problem, current alternatives, objections, proof, traffic source, and primary CTA. It then summarizes assumptions and waits for confirmation.

output_examples.input: A marketer provides a confirmed brief for a pricing page.
output_examples.output: The skill produces sectioned pricing copy, objection handling, a final CTA, three headline options, three CTA options, and notes on what each option tests.

output_examples.input: A team asks for stronger copy but has no testimonials or metrics.
output_examples.output: The skill avoids invented proof, marks proof gaps clearly, and suggests honest placeholders such as customer quote needed or usage metric needed.

best_practices: Provide audience research, objections, proof, and traffic source before requesting final copy.
Confirm or correct the copy brief before moving into drafting.
Test one headline or CTA message at a time so results are easier to interpret.
anti_patterns: Ask for persuasive copy without defining the reader or page goal.
Request fabricated statistics, testimonials, urgency, guarantees, or proof.
Combine several unrelated messages into one headline or CTA test.

faq.question: What kinds of pages does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports homepages, landing pages, pricing pages, feature pages, product pages, and about pages.

faq.question: Will it write copy immediately?
faq.answer: No. It gathers context, presents a copy brief, and waits for confirmation before drafting.

faq.question: Can it create testimonials or statistics?
faq.answer: No. It must not fabricate proof, testimonials, guarantees, statistics, or unsupported claims.

faq.question: Does it support A/B testing?
faq.answer: It creates headline and CTA variants with rationale, but it does not run experiments or analyze analytics data.

faq.question: Can it improve existing copy?
faq.answer: Yes. It can rewrite sections for clarity, benefits, specificity, honest claims, and stronger calls to action.

faq.question: What input improves results?
faq.answer: Share the target audience, page goal, offer, differentiator, objections, proof, traffic source, and awareness level.
